inclusion_criteria: Population: Human patients with breast, lung, or prostate cancer using data collected from two or more clinical centers (e.g., imaging, EHR, pathology, genomics).
Intervention: Federated learning approaches that train machine learning models on distributed, site-partitioned multi-centre oncology data.
Comparator: Includes a centralized machine learning baseline (single-centre or pooled/aggregated data) for comparison.

Outcomes/Design: Original empirical research reporting quantitative model generalizability and diagnostic/clinical performance for cancer detection, diagnosis, or precision medicine (e.g., AUROC, sensitivity/specificity, external/site-wise validation).
exclusion_criteria: No federated learning or no empirical multi-centre evaluation (e.g., centralized-only studies, methodological proposals without real multi-site data).
Population not relevant (does not include breast, lung, or prostate cancer patients) or relies only on non-human/phantom/synthetic data.
No centralized comparator model reported for performance comparison.
Does not report diagnostic/generalizability outcomes or is not focused on detection, diagnosis, or precision oncology (e.g., purely systems, privacy, or workflow papers without model performance).
screening_criteria.description: Screen studies that apply federated learning to multi-centre breast, lung, or prostate cancer data and compare against centralized ML, reporting generalizability and diagnostic/clinical performance in detection, diagnosis, or precision medicine.

included_articles.title: Federated Learning with Research Prototypes: Application to Multi-Center MRI-based Detection of Prostate Cancer with Diverse Histopathology.
Risk stratification for early-stage NSCLC progression: a federated learning framework with large-small model synergy.
